key_insight: Detonation-sprayed Ti/HAp functionally graded coatings on Ti-6Al-4V achieved a bioceramic surface with a controlled titanium-to-hydroxyapatite transition, with the smoother gradient showing the best adhesion.
engineering_relevance: These graded coatings are promising for load-bearing implant surfaces because they combine strong substrate bonding with a hydroxyapatite-rich outer layer for bioactivity.
method: Fabricated two Ti/HAp gradient architectures by varying the number and sequence of detonation-spraying cycles.
Characterized coating structure and composition using SEM, EDS, and Raman spectroscopy.
Measured adhesion strength using ASTM C633-13 tensile testing.
evidence_signal: EDS showed titanium concentrated near the interface and Ca/P enrichment toward the top layer, while Raman confirmed hydroxyapatite at 961 cm−1 and adhesion reached 45.78 ± 4.4 MPa and 52.32 ± 6.7 MPa.
watch_item: Results are promising, but long-term in vivo durability, wear, and corrosion behavior were not assessed in the abstract.
paper_title: Detonation Spraying of Functionally Graded Hydroxyapatite/Titanium Coatings on Ti–6Al–4V Alloy
